solute.description: This molecule is a linear, four-carbon dicarboxylic acid consisting of a saturated butane chain with terminal carboxyl groups at the C1 and C4 positions. The structure is characterized by its complete symmetry and the absence of any branching or cyclic components. It exhibits high polarity and extensive hydrogen bonding capacity, featuring four oxygen atoms that act as hydrogen bond acceptors and two hydroxyl groups serving as hydrogen bond donors. Its small molecular size and dual acidic functionality make it a compact and highly functionalized organic diacid.

solvent.description: This molecule, acetic acid, consists of a two-carbon aliphatic chain where a terminal methyl group is covalently bonded to a carboxylic acid functional group. The structure is characterized by an $sp^2$-hybridized carbonyl carbon that is simultaneously double-bonded to one oxygen and single-bonded to a hydroxyl group. It is a small, highly polar molecule with significant hydrogen bonding capacity, acting as both a hydrogen bond donor through the hydroxyl group and an acceptor via the oxygen lone pairs. Its distinctive nature arises from the compact carboxyl group, which allows for the formation of stable intermolecular dimers.
